title: Lescure-Meyniel Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: Every graph with chromatic number $k$ contains a weak immersion of the complete graph $K_{k}$.
context_text: Lescure and Meyniel suggested this conjecture in 1989 as a weakening of Hajós' conjecture, replacing vertex-disjoint subdivisions with edge-disjoint paths that may pass through branch vertices. It has been verified for $4\leq k\leq 6$ by DeVos, Kawarabayashi, Mohar, and Okamura, and remains open for $k\geq 7$.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for graphs with chromatic number $k$ and at most $(1.64-\varepsilon)k$ vertices (for any $\varepsilon>0$ and $k$ sufficiently large), and unconditionally for graphs with fewer than $1.4k-0.6$ vertices.

title: Albertson's Conjecture (Conjecture 1.3)
statement_text: Every graph $G$ with chromatic number $k$ satisfies $\mathrm{cr}(G)\geq\mathrm{cr}(K_{k})$.
context_text: Conjectured by Albertson in 2007, this asserts that among all graphs of a given chromatic number the complete graph minimises the crossing number. It vacuously holds for $k\leq 4$, is equivalent to the four colour theorem for $k=5$, has been confirmed for $k\leq 18$, and is open for $k\geq 19$.
paper_contribution: Shows the conjecture holds for all graphs of chromatic number $k$ that have at most $(1.64-o(1))k$ vertices, as an application of the authors' new result on weak immersions of complete graphs.
